description: Install elkjs as a server-side dependency and create a layout computation module that accepts a task dependency graph and returns node positions using ELK's layered algorithm with top-to-bottom direction
steps_to_verify: elkjs is listed in package.json dependencies
Layout module accepts { nodes: {id, width, height}[], edges: {source, target}[] } and returns { nodes: {id, x, y}[], edges: {source, target, sections}[] }
Layout uses ELK layered algorithm with direction TOP_DOWN
Node width is fixed at 200px, height at ~72px

description: Compute the critical path (longest chain from any root to any leaf by task count) and include it in the layout result as an ordered array of task IDs
steps_to_verify: Critical path is computed as the longest path through the DAG (by node count, not duration)
Result includes meta.critical_path as string[] of ordered task IDs
Disconnected subgraphs are handled — critical path is the longest across all subgraphs

description: Create a proj_graph_layout table storing a single-row JSON blob with node positions, edge data, and critical path metadata
steps_to_verify: Table proj_graph_layout exists with columns: id (PK, always 'singleton'), layout_json TEXT, updated_at TEXT
layout_json shape: { nodes: { [task_id]: {x, y, width, height} }, edges: { source, target }[], meta: { critical_path: string[], direction: string } }
Layout blob is atomically replaced on each recomputation

description: Subscribe to graph-affecting events via eventBus 'event.committed' and recompute layout with 200ms debounce
steps_to_verify: Layout recomputes on: task.created, task.dependency.set, task.status_changed, task.archived, task.unblocked
Rapid-fire events within 200ms are batched into a single recomputation
Layout computation runs async, outside the event transaction
Errors in layout computation are logged but do not crash the server

description: Add GET /api/projections/graph_layout endpoint that serves the layout blob, with optional prd_id filter
steps_to_verify: GET /api/projections/graph_layout returns the full layout JSON blob
GET /api/projections/graph_layout?prd_id=X returns layout filtered to tasks belonging to that PRD
Response includes task status and title alongside coordinates so the client doesn't need a separate fetch

description: Install @xyflow/react as a client-side dependency for graph rendering
steps_to_verify: @xyflow/react is listed in package.json dependencies
React Flow CSS is imported in the graph component

description: Create a DependencyGraph component that renders the server-computed layout using React Flow with custom task nodes
steps_to_verify: Component fetches layout from /api/projections/graph_layout
Nodes are positioned using server-provided x,y coordinates (no client-side layout computation)
Graph supports zoom and pan via React Flow built-in controls
Graph fits to view on initial render

description: Create a custom React Flow node component: fixed-width (~200px), truncated title (2 lines max), status colour as left border, attempt counter badge
steps_to_verify: Node shows truncated task title (max 2 lines with ellipsis)

Left border colour reflects actionable-centric status: green=ready (draft/queued unblocked), yellow=in-flight (running/revising/paused), red=failed (rejected), blue=done (merged/approved/awaiting_review/awaiting_merge), grey=blocked/archived
Attempt counter shows as 'N/M' badge (current/max)
Done tasks (merged/archived) render at 30% opacity

description: Render dependency edges with done-task dimming: edges to done tasks are faint dashed lines, active edges are solid
steps_to_verify: Edges to tasks with status merged/approved/awaiting_merge/archived render as faint dashed lines (~30% opacity)
Edges between active tasks render as solid lines at full opacity
Critical path edges are visually distinct (thicker or highlighted colour) when critical path is shown

description: Highlight the critical path: nodes and edges on the critical path get a distinct visual treatment
steps_to_verify: Critical path nodes and edges are highlighted with a distinct colour or glow
Critical path data comes from meta.critical_path in the layout blob (no client computation)
Non-critical-path elements remain visible but are visually secondary when critical path is highlighted

description: Clicking a node opens a condensed floating detail card showing title, status, attempt count, action buttons, and a 'View details' link
steps_to_verify: Clicking a node shows a floating card positioned near the node
Card shows: full title, status pill, attempt count (N/M), and relevant action buttons (Start/Retry/Kill based on status)
'View details' link switches to list mode with that task selected
Clicking outside the card or pressing Escape dismisses it
Only one card visible at a time

description: Add a list/graph toggle in the tasks screen. Graph mode renders as a full-width overlay with a minimal status bar
steps_to_verify: Toggle button is visible in both list and graph modes
List mode renders the existing TaskListSidebar + TaskDetailPane layout
Graph mode renders as full-width overlay replacing the sidebar+detail layout
Status bar shows: toggle button, PRD filter dropdown, compact status counts (N ready, N running, N done, N blocked)
Toggle state persists across re-renders (useState, not URL-based)

description: PRD filter dropdown in graph status bar filters the graph to show only tasks from the selected PRD
steps_to_verify: Dropdown defaults to 'All' showing all tasks
Selecting a PRD filters to only that PRD's tasks and their dependency edges
Standalone tasks (no prd_id) appear under 'Standalone' option
Filter triggers a re-fetch of the layout endpoint with prd_id parameter

description: Visual QA: verify the graph renders correctly with real task data from the orchestrator database
steps_to_verify: Create 5+ tasks with a diamond dependency pattern (A->B, A->C, B->D, C->D) and verify layout has no overlapping nodes
Verify done tasks and their edges are visually dimmed
Verify critical path highlighting visually traces the longest chain
Verify the condensed detail card appears on node click and action buttons work
Verify toggling between list and graph mode preserves selected task context
